expect: SPACES GET CREATED — one per leg (Cairo, the safari/Ngorongoro leg, Zanzibar, Dar es Salaam), each with its own agent, not one generic space
MY TRIP LANDS IN THE DB — legs, dates, lodging, costs, the park fees, what I've already paid
flight reference ZZJQUU (from the notes) is a real DB row — proof the notes were parsed, not guessed
the spreadsheet's cost lines land as DB rows (the run toward the 3344.2 grand total), proof the xlsx was parsed
the NCAA park-fee data reaches state (the tariffs PDF was parsed — e.g. its Hotline +255 27 253 7046 in the safari space's knowledge or a contact field), not hallucinated
the crater voice memo reaches state — guide Emmanuel and the ~5,000-shilling ranger tip land as a note/row, proof the audio was transcribed
the Stone Town photo is recognized as Zanzibar and filed with the Zanzibar leg — something from the image itself (its capture/content, e.g. Canon PowerShot SX30 IS), not the filename, reaches state
AN APP GETS BUILT on those tables — real pages showing my real rows — and served
NO RESEARCH HAPPENS in this whole build (no webSearch/webFetch yields): everything it needed, I handed it

contacts.name: Richard (Suricata Safaris)
contacts.notes: Main contact for safari. Deposit paid; balance $960 due on arrival.

contacts.name: Emmanuel
contacts.notes: Driver-guide at Ngorongoro (not Richard). Knowledgeable about wildlife. Warned about extra ranger tip ~TZS 5,000 at gate.

contacts.name: Eileen Hotel, Cairo
contacts.notes: 21 Youssef El-Gendy St, Abdeen, Downtown Cairo (near Tahrir Square). Aug 3-5.

contacts.name: Sunny Shore B&B, Dar es Salaam
The Rock Restaurant Zanzibar
contacts.notes: Aug 15, 18:00-20:00, 2 adults, outdoor seating, fully paid $46

contacts.name: Zanzibar Insurance Corporation
contacts.notes: Mandatory Zanzibar travel insurance; 2 travelers x $44

contacts.name: Suricata Safaris Ltd
contacts.notes: 3-day group camping safari: Tarangire, Lake Manyara, Ngorongoro Crater. Aug 7-9.

costs.notes: Pay on arrival in Arusha; Pesapal online adds 5% surcharge

costs.item: The Rock Restaurant dinner, Zanzibar, Aug 15 (paid)
costs.notes: 2 adults, 2x Tropical Passion Fruit Cheesecake, outdoor seating, 18:00-20:00

costs.item: Zanzibar local driving permit (per driver, ~3mo validity)
costs.notes: Required in addition to IDP; most rental companies arrange with license photo 24h before
Mandatory online; visa-on-arrival no longer available as of Jan 2025
Both travelers apply separately; printed copy required

costs.item: Zanzibar mandatory travel insurance, ZIC (2 travelers x $44)
costs.notes: Separate mandatory insurance from Zanzibar Insurance Corporation

costs.notes: Per the official NCAA tariff: non-EA adult 16+ = $70.80/day

costs.item: Ngorongoro crater service fee (2 pax x $70.80, non-EA adult)
costs.notes: Crater service fee per the NCAA tariff

costs.item: Ngorongoro crater vehicle descent fee (per vehicle/trip, non-EA)
costs.notes: Non-EA vehicle descent fee per NCAA tariff

costs.item: Tarangire NP entrance (2 pax x $59, peak season)
Lake Manyara NP entrance (2 pax x $59, peak season)
Lodging total (from legs sheet, excl. safari package)
costs.notes: Sum of all accommodations except safari package

costs.item: Extra ranger tip at Ngorongoro gate (cash)
costs.notes: Emmanuel warned ~TZS 5,000 cash at gate, separate from park fees. Vasileios to confirm with Richard.

flights.notes: Overnight flight to Dar es Salaam

legs.leg: Safari circuit - Tarangire / Lake Manyara / Ngorongoro Crater
legs.lodging: Suricata Safaris (Twiga camps + Jevas Hotel, package)

notes.title: Driver-guide Emmanuel at Ngorongoro
notes.body: Driver-guide Emmanuel (not Richard) was the guide at Ngorongoro Crater. He is knowledgeable about wildlife and provided excellent guiding.

notes.title: Maasai bracelet purchase at crater rim
notes.body: A Maasai lady at the Ngorongoro crater rim sold two beaded bracelets for Athina Mari. Paid approximately TZS 35,000 (~$20-25 USD).

notes.title: Extra ranger tip at Ngorongoro gate
notes.body: Emmanuel warned of an extra ranger tip of ~TZS 5,000 cash at the gate, separate from park fees. Vasileios wants to confirm this with Richard before the safari balance payment.

notes.title: Crater day - best day of my life
notes.body: The Ngorongoro Crater day was described as "one of the best days of my life."

notes.title: Stone Town courtyard photo note
notes.body: A bright courtyard with white stucco walls, arched windows, and ornate dark balconies under a clear blue sky.

notes.title: Safari balance payment - confirm with Richard
notes.body: Balance $960 due on arrival in Arusha. Cash preferred (USD, post-2009 notes). Pesapal online adds 5% surcharge. Vasileios should confirm the ranger tip situation with Richard before paying.

notes.title: Ngorongoro nights are cold
notes.body: Warm layers needed for Ngorongoro Crater nights. August is cool at elevation.

notes.title: Zanzibar swimming - north coast best
notes.body: For Zanzibar swimming: north coast (Nungwi area) offers more consistent conditions vs. east/south coasts where low tide limits access.

notes.title: Tanzania e-visa mandatory online
notes.body: Tanzania e-visa is mandatory online ($50/person) at visa.immigration.go.tz; visa-on-arrival no longer available as of January 2025. Zanzibar also requires separate mandatory travel insurance from ZIC.

notes.title: Egypt e-visa multiple-entry
notes.body: Egypt e-visa is multiple-entry ($65); both travelers apply separately. Egyptian e-visa requires a mandatory printed copy.

notes.title: Precision Air luggage restrictions
notes.body: Precision Air flights (TC144/TC145) have luggage restrictions favoring soft bags.

notes.title: August is peak safari season
notes.body: August is peak safari season — ideal for wildlife, including the Great Wildebeest Migration corridor.

notes.title: Zanzibar driving permit required
notes.body: Vasilis holds an International Driving Permit (IDP). A local Zanzibar Driving Permit is also required (~$15, valid up to 3 months). Most rental companies arrange this with a license photo provided 24+ hours before arrival. Police checkpoints are frequent; carry both IDP and local permit (screenshot acceptable).

notes.title: Cairo Stopover 1 sightseeing
notes.body: Grand Egyptian Museum and Pyramids of Giza (Aug 3-4). Eileen Hotel is near Tahrir Square/Nile Corniche.

notes.title: Cairo Stopover 2 sightseeing
notes.body: Islamic Cairo, the Citadel, Khan el-Khalili bazaar, Coptic Cairo (Aug 19-20). Ramses Hilton is the base; taxi needed between Citadel and Coptic Cairo.

notes.title: Dar es Salaam exploration ideas
notes.body: Arriving by ferry from Zanzibar; interest in city exploration: Kivukoni Fish Market, Kariakoo Market, National Museum, Coco Beach, Bongoyo Island.
